meta.feed_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

items.title: Israeli police inspect damage on the road after missile impact
items.summary: Police inspect a strike site in Tel Aviv after a missile hit the city.

items.title: Iran attacks cause fire in Kuwait, Bahrain; kill man in UAE
items.summary: Shrapnel from an Iranian drone interception kills a Bangladeshi national in the UAE's Fujairah city.

items.title: What is NASA’s Artemis II moon mission, and when will it launch?
items.summary: For the first time in more than 50 years, humans will travel around the moon to test deep-space life-support systems.

items.title: Spain’s FA condemns Islamophobic chants during game with Egypt
items.summary: Pre-World Cup friendly in Spain marred by racist and anti-Muslim chants by the home fans.

items.title: Iran war: What is happening on day 33 of US-Israel attacks?
items.summary: Trump says US could leave Iran war in 2-3 weeks without a deal; Iran’s FM Araghchi says he has no faith in talks.

items.title: Iraq defeat Bolivia 2-1 to qualify for FIFA World Cup 2026
items.summary: Goals from Ali al-Hamadi and Aymen Hussein send Iraq to their first FIFA World Cup since 1986.

items.title: First Black astronaut candidate ‘proud’ Victor Glover will make history
items.summary: First Black astronaut candidate Ed Dwight says he’s “really proud” of Victor Glover who’s set to make history.

items.title: Residents inspect drone damage near Erbil airport
items.summary: Residents living near Erbil's international airport in northern Iraq, say falling drone debris has damaged properties.

items.title: American journalist kidnapped in Baghdad
items.summary: American freelance journalist Shelly Kittleson has been kidnapped in broad daylight in Baghdad.

items.title: Judge halts Trump ballroom project; Trump says work can go on
items.summary: Donald Trump seemed to suggest work on the $400 million White House ballroom could continue despite a judge’s order.

items.title: Turkiye qualifies for World Cup after 24-year wait
items.summary: Fans celebrated as Turkiye ended a 24-year wait to secure a spot in the 2026 World Cup.

items.title: Global protests condemning Israel’s new death penalty law for Palestinians
items.summary: Protesters are condemning Israel's new bill that makes the death penalty the default punishment for some Palestinians.

items.title: Al-Sharaa says Syria to stay out of war on Iran unless attacked
items.summary: '14 years of war is enough for Syria': Ahmed al-Sharaa says Syria will remain outside the US-Israeli war on Iran.

items.title: Bosnia fans celebrate as team qualifies for World Cup, knocking Italy out
items.summary: Supporters celebrated as Bosnia and Herzegovina beat Italy in a penalty shootout to secure a spot in the 2026 World Cup.

items.title: Emergency workers search for victims of Israeli airstrikes on Beirut
items.summary: Emergency workers searched through rubble in Beirut after Israeli airstrikes hit the Jnah neighbourhood.

items.title: ‘A law of death that only applies to Palestinians’
items.summary: Protesters demanded UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer act, not just condemn, Israel’s death penalty law for Palestinians.

items.title: Judge temporarily halts Trump’s $400m White House ballroom project
items.summary: District Judge Richard Leon says construction has to stop until Congress provides statutory authorisation.

items.title: Trump tells allies ‘get your own oil’, says Iran war could end in 2-3 weeks
items.summary: Under pressure as domestic petrol prices soar, US president says no deal needed to end war on Iran.

items.title: Iran war live: ‘Missile from Iran’ hits oil tanker off Qatar’s coast
items.summary: One of three missiles launched from Iran hit an oil tanker off Qatar's coast, no injuries reported.

items.title: DR Congo beat Jamaica to reach World Cup via intercontinental finals
items.summary: Former Manchester United defender Axel Tuanzebe scores in extra time as DR Congo beat Jamaica 1-0 in qualifying playoff.

items.title: Trump says US will be leaving Iran in two to three weeks
items.summary: Speaking to reporters in the Oval Office, US President Donald Trump says US could leave Iran “in two or three weeks."

items.title: Pope Leo urges Trump to end war on Iran
items.summary: Pope Leo XIV has urged US President Donald Trump to find an “off-ramp” from the US and Israel’s war on Iran.

items.title: After Strait of Hormuz opens, turmoil would still last months, analysts say
items.summary: Effective shutdown of key waterway could lead to permanent changes in how shipping industry does business.

items.title: US exempts Gulf of Mexico oil drillers from endangered species protections
items.summary: The Trump administration says protections would diminish US energy competitiveness amid the ongoing war on Iran.

items.title: EU warns oil and gas prices won’t quickly return to normal after Iran war
items.summary: The EU’s energy commissioner has warned oil and gas prices will not return to normal any time soon.
